title: crataegus coccinea var. pringlei
intro: Crataegus coccinea var. pringlei, also known as Pringle's hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and improving circulation. Pringle's hawthorn has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including heart failure, angina, and high blood pressure, due to its high content of flavonoids and procyanidins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide additional health benefits.
